title: 10 best herbal capsules for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for jet lag are a natural remedy designed to alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, which occur when traveling across time zones. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as Ginkgo biloba, Valerian root, and Ashwagandha, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue. By taking these herbal capsules before bedtime during travel, individuals can improve their ability to adjust to new time zones and wake up feeling refreshed. For example, taking Passionflower or Lemon Balm capsules can help with anxiety and insomnia associated with jet lag, allowing travelers to get a good night's sleep and start their day feeling revitalized. This natural approach has improved the lives of many frequent flyers by reducing the discomfort and disruption caused by jet lag.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with jet lag because they promote a restful night's sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new time zone more effectively. The herbal extract in valerian capsules has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can be exacerbated by travel fatigue. By promoting deep relaxation and improving sleep quality, valerian capsules can help minimize the discomfort associated with jet lag, such as fatigue, irritability, and insomnia, making it easier to adjust to your new surroundings.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian's sedative and relaxant properties can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite disruptions from travel.
GABA-Enhancing Activity: Valerian root has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which helps to regulate sleep-wake cycles and promote relaxation.
Anxiolytic Effects: Valerian's anxiolytic properties can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress related to travel, making it easier to adapt to new environments and get a good night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, making them an effective ingredient for promoting relaxation and reducing stress related to jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Containing high levels of valerenic acid, which helps regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep disrupted by travel across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 200g of dried valeriana officinalis root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered valeriana root with 100g of gelatin in a bowl until well combined and uniform.
Add 50ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved and the mixture forms a paste.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the valeriana-gelatin mixture and seal them immediately using an encapsulation machine or by hand.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use, taking one capsule 30 minutes before bedtime to alleviate jet lag symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a calming compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of jet lag. By taking lemon balm capsules before bedtime during travel, individuals can promote a restful night's sleep, even when crossing multiple time zones. Additionally, lemon balm's natural sedative properties help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, making it easier to adjust to the new time zone.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel fatigue, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag.

GABA-enhancing properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that enhance the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, which can aid in adjusting to a new sleep schedule and reducing symptoms of insomnia associated with jet lag.

Stress-reducing adaptogenic effects: Lemon balm's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stressors, such as time changes, by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting feelings of calmness and relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of anxiety and fatigue often experienced during travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Flowers: Contain rosmarinic acid, a natural antioxidant that soothes digestive issues and calms anxiety associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for the desired quantity of dried material.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature.
Grind 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground melissa officinalis powder with 50 milliliters of vegetable glycerin and 20 milliliters of water to create a smooth extract.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the melissa officinalis extract mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a natural combination of herbs that work in harmony to regulate the body's internal clock. The passionflower, which is the primary ingredient, has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and stress, which are often symptoms of jet lag. Additionally, maypop contains calming agents like valerian root, lemon balm, and chamomile that help to reduce symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue commonly experienced during travel across time zones. By promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness, maypop capsules can help the body adapt to new routines and rhythms more easily, minimizing the effects of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to promote relaxation, calmness, and reduce anxiety associated with travel stress and jet lag due to its sedative and anxiolytic effects.
Vitex agnus-castus: Supports the body's natural circadian rhythms and helps regulate sleep patterns by modulating the production of hormones such as melatonin and cortisol.
Melatonin-rich Ashwagandha extract: Enhances the body's ability to adapt to new time zones by promoting a regular sleep-wake cycle, reducing jet lag symptoms like insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, the roots of Maypop are valued for their adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high concentration of fatty acids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to promote relaxation, calmness, and improved sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning for maximum potency and essential oils.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by rinsing them gently with cold filtered water to remove impurities and debris.
Dry the cleaned flowers using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to maintain their medicinal properties.
Grind dried passiflora incarnata flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding 2 ounces of herb material per serving size.
Fill gelatin capsules with a precise dosage of 500 milligrams each containing the ground passiflora incarnata powder and seal immediately to preserve potency and freshness within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic herbs that work to regulate the body's internal clock. The capsules help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. By promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, maidenhair tree capsules can ease the symptoms of jet lag, allowing travelers to adjust to new time zones more quickly and comfortably. This natural remedy is especially beneficial for those who experience frequent or long-distance travel.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress caused by travel and jet lag, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.

Improves circulation and oxygenation: By enhancing blood flow to the brain and other organs, Ginkgo biloba capsules can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, dizziness, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag.

Neuroprotective and adaptogenic effects: Ginkgo biloba's neuroprotective compounds can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, reducing the negative impact of time changes on sleep-wake cycles and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have been shown to regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves from reputable sources measuring 250mg per capsule.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 500mg of gelatin capsules in a clean glass bowl.
Fill each gelatin capsule with the mixture and seal immediately to maintain potency preparing 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue skullcap capsules helps with jet lag because they contain Scutellaria lateriflora, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress. By taking blue skullcap capsules, travelers can alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness. The herb works by calming the nervous system, allowing the body to adjust to new time zones more easily and reducing the disruption caused by long-distance travel.
remedy_properties: Scopolamine: Blue skullcap contains scopolamine, a natural alkaloid that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and can reduce symptoms of insomnia and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid compound found in blue skullcap, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with traveling across time zones, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Acacetin: Acacetin, another flavonoid present in blue skullcap, has been shown to have sedative and hypnotic effects, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of insomnia, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat insomnia and other sleep disorders, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest scutellaria laxa leaves and flowers in early morning for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 3-4 hours or air dry for 7 days.
Grind 200g of dried scutellaria laxa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100g of silica gel and 50ml of vegetable glycerin to fill capsules.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture, ensuring each capsule contains 250mg of scutellaria laxa extract and 125mg of silica gel.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic herbs like Ashwagandha, Rhodiola, and Ginseng that work in harmony to regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms. These herbs help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by time changes, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. By regulating cortisol levels and increasing melatonin production, oats capsules help to realign the body's internal clock with the new local time zone, ensuring a smoother transition and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Herbal oats capsules contain saponins, which have sedative effects, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Antioxidant properties: Oats are rich in antioxidants, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.

Digestive soothing properties: The fiber-rich content of oats helps soothe digestive issues often experienced during jet lag, such as constipation or diarrhea, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in beta-glucans, which help to regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms such as insomnia and restlessness caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried avena sativa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered oat herb with 125 milliliters of cold pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar for 30 seconds.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius in a double boiler for 1 hour and 15 minutes to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the cooled infused oil and seal them immediately for use as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a natural sedative that promotes relaxation and reduces stress. When traveling across time zones, our bodies can struggle to adjust to the new sleep-wake cycle, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other symptoms of jet lag. The calming effects of hops capsules help to regulate the body's internal clock, allowing for a smoother transition and reducing the likelihood of disrupted sleep patterns. As a result, individuals taking hops capsules may experience improved sleep quality and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal hops capsules contain adaptogens that help regulate the body's response to stress, which can aid in adjusting to new time zones and minimizing the effects of jet lag.

GABA-Mimetic Effects: Hops contain compounds that mimic the action of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite time zone changes.

Sedative Properties: Herbal hops capsules have sedative properties that can help calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, which is common when traveling across multiple time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective against jet lag-induced fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried hops flowers at peak potency and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Grind the dried hops flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvest.
Mix 1000mg of ground hops powder with 500mg of vitamin b complex capsules in a small bowl to create a synergistic blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the hops-vitamin b complex blend and seal them immediately using a capsule filler or a funnel.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until opened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and regulate sleep patterns. The calming effects of lavender help to ease the body's natural response to time changes, making it easier to fall asleep at night and stay awake during the day. Additionally, lavender's adaptogenic properties allow it to adjust to an individual's unique needs, providing a gentle and soothing aid for overcoming jet lag.
remedy_properties: Calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. This can lead to a more restful night's sleep and a smoother transition to the new local time.

Melatonin regulation: Lavender has been shown to help regulate melatonin levels in the body, which is essential for establishing a regular sleep-wake cycle. By supporting natural melatonin production, lavender capsules can help your body adjust to the new time zone faster.

Sedative properties: Lavender's sedative effects can also help promote relaxation and reduce restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep during travel or when arriving at a destination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of insomnia.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has been found to promote relaxation and calmness, aiding in the adjustment to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 60°c for 4 hours or until they crumble easily between fingers.
Grind 10g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of ground lavandula angustifolia powder and seal the caps securely.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with jet lag because of its natural ability to regulate digestive rhythms and reduce inflammation. When traveling across time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to new sleep patterns, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Herbal peppermint capsules contain menthol, which helps synchronize the body's circadian rhythms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. By calming the digestive system and soothing the mind, peppermint capsules can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, allowing travelers to recover faster and feel more refreshed during their journey.
remedy_properties: Digestive soothing properties: Peppermint oil's ability to relax muscles in the digestive tract can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea caused by jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jet lag, including headaches, fatigue, and muscle soreness, allowing the body to adapt more quickly to a new environment.

Stress-relieving properties: The calming effects of peppermint oil can help mitigate the stress and anxiety often experienced during travel, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, which is essential for recovering from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help regulate body clock and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: Peppermint barks contain menthol, which helps stimulate digestive system and alleviate nausea associated with travel across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when they are most potent for their active compounds.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or until crispy brittle texture achieved.
Grind dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve to ensure consistency.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250mg of the powdered peppermint and seal promptly to maintain potency.
Store prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with jet lag because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which are often heightened when traveling across different time zones. The herbal blend in these capsules has a calming effect on the body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of rest during travel. Additionally, the lime oil present in the capsules has been shown to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of jet lag-related fatigue, making it an effective natural remedy for overcoming the discomforts of time zone changes.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal lime capsules contain adaptogens like ashwagandha and ginseng, which help the body adjust to new time zones by normalizing cortisol levels and reducing stress response, thus minimizing the effects of jet lag.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidant compounds in herbal lime capsules, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms like fatigue and irritability.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Herbal lime capsules often contain soothing herbs like peppermint and ginger, which can help alleviate digestive issues commonly experienced during long-haul flights, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain compounds that help regulate circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Bark: The bark of the lime tree has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate fatigue and stress caused by travel across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tilia x europaea from local farmers or online retailers and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Measure out 200mg of dried tilia x europaea flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground tilia x europaea powder with 50mg of dried lemon balm leaves and 25mg of dried valerian root in a small bowl for a total of 275mg herbal blend.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal blend, using a capsule filling machine or manually spooning the mixture into each capsule shell, then seal the capsules tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container and label them with the date prepared, ingredients used, and recommended dosage of 2-3 capsules per day for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work synergistically to regulate the body's internal clock. The adaptogenic properties of hawthorn help to reduce stress and anxiety, common symptoms associated with travel across time zones. Additionally, the cardio-protective compounds in hawthorn have been shown to improve circulation and oxygenation of the brain, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness. This allows the body to adjust to new time zones more easily, minimizing disruptions to daily routines.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's vasodilating properties can help to relax blood vessels, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag and promoting a sense of calmness.
Cardioprotective effects: Hawthorn's cardio-protective compounds can help to regulate heartbeat and blood pressure, which can be disrupted by jet lag, and promote overall cardiovascular health.

Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn's adaptogenic compounds can help the body adapt to new time zones and environments, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and digestive issues often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and stress, making them suitable for alleviating symptoms associated with jet lag.

Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain flavonoids and oligomeric procyanidins that help to regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial in addressing fatigue and disorientation caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in late summer.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried crataegus monogyna into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of powdered crataegus monogyna with 50 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction.
Encapsulate the decoction in gelatin capsules with a dosage of 500 milligrams taken thrice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with jet lag because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have adaptogenic properties. When taken regularly, it can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by time zone changes. The herb also has anti-inflammatory effects, which can alleviate fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag. Additionally, licorice capsules contain flavonoids, which can help improve sleep quality and duration, making it easier to adjust to a new timezone.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal licorice capsules can help the body adapt to changes in sleep patterns, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, anxiety, and insomnia by promoting hormonal balance and stress relief.

Digestive Soothing Effects: Licorice root has been shown to calm digestive issues like nausea, bloating, and stomach upset often caused by travel-related food sensitivities or altered eating habits, allowing for a smoother transition to new time zones.

Antioxidant Properties: Licorice root's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches, muscle tension, and general malaise, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and stress.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root powder measuring precisely one part by weight.
Mix two grams of licorice root powder with 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard solids instantly.
Capsule the filtered liquid using empty gelatin capsules containing 250 milligrams per serving size.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two weeks promptly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arabica capsules helps with jet lag because they contain carefully selected herbs that promote a natural adjustment to new time zones. The unique blend of botanicals in these capsules works to regulate the body's internal clock, reducing the effects of fatigue and disorientation often experienced during long-distance travel. By stimulating the production of melatonin and other essential hormones, Arabica capsules help restore a sense of balance and energy, making it easier to adjust to new schedules and environments.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Arabica's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, including time zone changes, by reducing cortisol levels and increasing resilience.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of Arabica help protect against oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and headaches.
Melatonin Regulation: Some herbal Arabica capsules contain melatonin-regulating compounds that help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, allowing for a smoother transition to local time.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Arabica plant contain a high concentration of caffeine and other bioactive compounds, which help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Arabica have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat insomnia and other sleep disorders, making them a key component in medicinal capsules for jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality coffea arabica beans from a reputable source and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind the dried coffee beans into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve an optimal particle size.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground coffee powder with two teaspoons of valerian root powder and half teaspoon of chamomile flower extract in a small bowl to create a blended formula.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blended formula, ensuring each capsule contains the exact same dose of 1.5 grams and seals them tightly using a capsule filler machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for longer storage to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with jet lag because they promote digestive health, which is often disrupted by long-distance travel. The capsules contain a natural herb that stimulates digestion and alleviates bloating, cramps, and other discomforts associated with traveling across time zones. By regulating bowel movements, fennel capsules help the body adjust to new rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and irritability. As a result, travelers can enjoy a smoother transition into their destination's time zone and feel more refreshed and focused during their stay.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Fennel's natural oils can help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort that often accompany jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by air travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Fennel's anti-spasmodic properties can help calm an upset stomach and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of nausea, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal discomforts often associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, fennel roots help regulate body rhythms and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a natural sedative that promotes relaxation and helps adjust to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from your garden or a local botanical store.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare plant material in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of ground foeniculum vulgare with 250mg gelatin capsules and fill with distilled water.
Allow the mixture to set for at least 30 minutes before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with jet lag because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have a regulating effect on the body's circadian rhythms. When traveling across time zones, our natural sleep-wake cycles are disrupted, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other symptoms of jet lag. Milk thistle capsules may help normalize these rhythms by promoting relaxation and reducing oxidative stress, allowing your body to adjust to its new environment more quickly and easily.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can disrupt the body's natural rhythms and make it harder to adjust to new time zones.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that help to protect the liver from damage caused by changes in diet and environment during travel, allowing the body to adapt more easily to new environments.
Sterols: Help to regulate liver function and improve digestion, which can become disrupted due to changes in diet and water intake during travel, making it easier to adjust to a new timezone.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid that helps protect the liver and reduce oxidative stress caused by jet lag-induced disruptions.
Roots: Contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and helps regulate circadian rhythms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried silybum marianum herb from reputable sources ensuring freshness.
Grind the dried herb into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Mix 50g of powdered herb with 250ml of cold distilled water in a clean glass bowl for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes allowing for efficient extraction of silymarin compounds.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the cooled herbal liquid extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with jet lag because they contain hyperforin, a natural compound that regulates melatonin levels in the body. Melatonin is responsible for governing our sleep-wake cycles, and when our bodies are exposed to different time zones, it can be challenging for them to adjust. Hyperforin works by stimulating the production of melatonin, helping to regulate our circadian rhythms and ease the symptoms of jet lag. As a result, taking St John's wort capsules before traveling can help reduce fatigue, improve sleep quality, and make it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: St. John's Wort contains compounds that help regulate stress hormones and promote adaptability to new environments, reducing feelings of disorientation and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort capsules may help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet travel, which can contribute to symptoms like headaches, insomnia, and muscle soreness.

Serotonin regulation: St. John's Wort is known for its ability to regulate serotonin levels, a neurotransmitter that helps control mood, appetite, and sleep-wake cycles. By supporting healthy serotonin production, St. John's Wort may help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a key compound responsible for regulating sleep patterns and reducing symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Contain hypericin, a bioflavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate fatigue and insomnia associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hypericum perforatum flowers in quantities of 20 grams.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground hypericum powder with gelatin capsules according to the ratio 250 milligrams per capsule fill.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the hypericum mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with jet lag because they contain Berberine, a natural compound that regulates circadian rhythms. When taken as directed, barberry capsules can help reset the body's internal clock, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive discomfort. The herbal remedy also promotes relaxation and calms the mind, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. By supporting the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, barberry capsules can help alleviate the disruptions caused by travel across different time zones.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Barberry contains berberine, a compound that has been shown to regulate body rhythms and improve sleep quality by increasing the production of melatonin, the hormone responsible for regulating our circadian clocks.

Vasodilatory properties: Barberry's vasodilatory effects can help relieve symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, headaches, and insomnia by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the body.

Antioxidant activity: Barberry's rich antioxidant content helps protect cells from oxidative stress caused by travel-related stressors like changes in diet, environment, and sleep patterns, which can contribute to feelings of disorientation and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant contain a high concentration of berberine, an alkaloid compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help regulate circadian rhythms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the barberry plant are rich in berberine and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive disturbances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried berberis vulagris roots and stems from a trusted source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Measure out 250mg of the powdered herb per capsule and mix it with 100mg of filler material such as rice flour or potato starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herb and filler mixture and seal them tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before taking 2-3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild rose capsules helps with jet lag because they contain the natural essence of rose petals, which has been traditionally used to alleviate stress and fatigue. The capsules help regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing the symptoms associated with jet lag such as insomnia, anxiety, and disorientation. By taking these capsules, travelers can minimize the effects of time zone changes and feel refreshed and revitalized upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Wild rose capsules contain adaptogens, such as rose petals, which help the body adapt to changes in environment and rhythm, reducing stress and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in wild rose capsules, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by jet lag, promoting a smoother transition to new time zones.

Stress-relieving property: Wild rose capsules contain calming compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, helping alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild rose plant are used to make medicinal capsules due to their calming effects on the nervous system, helping to regulate sleep patterns.
Bark: The bark of the wild rose plant is used to create capsules that can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers and dry them immediately for 7 days in a clean, well-ventilated area.
Grind dried rosa rugosa flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 1000mg of valerian root powder and 500mg of lemon balm leaf powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ready for use when jet lag strikes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal capsules is to opt for a blend that combines natural sleep promoters like valerian root, chamomile, and lemon balm. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps regulate your body's internal clock, while GABA promotes relaxation. Consume the capsules 30 minutes before bedtime, allowing the herbs to work together to ease jet lag symptoms, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal capsules are circadian rhythm disorders, seasonal affective disorder, and fatigue. Herbal capsules such as those containing valerian root, ashwagandha, and melatonin can help regulate sleep patterns, alleviate symptoms of insomnia, and reduce feelings of exhaustion and stress. Additionally, herbs like St. John's Wort and Ginkgo biloba may help improve mood and cognitive function. These natural remedies can provide a gentle and non-invasive approach to addressing these common health issues.
